hypothesis: If a ∆-learning approach is used to correct systematic errors in computationally inexpensive GFN1-xTB band gap calculations using XGBoost models trained on structural descriptors, then the resulting predictions will more accurately reproduce high-level DFT (PBE and HSE06) band gaps for metal-organic frameworks than either direct machine learning models trained on DFT targets alone or deep learning benchmarks, because the semi-empirical method captures the dominant physics of the electronic structure while the correction model need only learn a smaller, more systematic residual error.
